titleStmt.title: SII 8.303: original edition by K.V. Subrahmanya Aiyer
No. 303. (A.R. No. 30 of 1903). ON A PILLAR OF THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME TEMPLE.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara
nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu makiḻat tan=ti ruppa
tumalar mannavar cūṭa manniya vurimaiyāl
maṇimuṭi cūṭic ceṅkol cenṟu ticai
toṟum vaḷarppa veṅkali nīṅki meyyaṟa
n taḻaippak kaliṅka meriyak kaṭarmalaina
ṭātti valaṅko ḷāḻivarai yāḻi tiriya Irucuṭaraḷavu
m Orukuṭai niḻaṟṟi virasiṁhā sanattu mukkokki
ḻānaṭikaḷoṭum viṟṟirun=ta ruḷiya kopparakecari
panma rāna tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvikkiramacoḻa
tevaṟkku yāṇṭu 7vatu siṁha nāyaṟṟu Aparapakṣattu E
kādaśi yun tiruvātiraiyum peṟṟa viyāḻakkiḻamai
nāḷ rājarājavaḷanāṭṭut tirumuṉaippāṭi Aviya
nūrnāṭṭu brahma tecam Iraṇatiraccaturvetimaṅkalattu
ma hāsabhai yom Ivvūrt tirumeṟkoyilāna
niyāyaparipālanaviṇṇakarāḻvār koyilile
kūṭṭak kuṟaivaṟak kūṭiyirun tu nammur yāṇṭu 6
vatu kaṭamaittaṭ ṭuṇṭāy Ittaṭṭukku samutāya
māna nilattile ciṟitu nilam viṟṟākilum kaṭamai
ttaṭṭup pokkaṟukkaveṇu menṟu ma hāsapai
yom sammatittu Innāṭṭuk kiḻāmurnāṭṭu
Ātirājamaṅkilliyapurattu viyāpāri pallavan cileṭṭi
mātevaṭikaḷ Aracu Ivvūrt tiru
Emperumānukku tiruman=ti rat tirupponakaamutupaṭikku
can=ti rātittavaṟ cālaic celvatāka In=ta māte
vaṭikaḷ Aracinpe(r)rāl nāṅkaḷ viṟṟukkuṭutta
nilamāvatu . Eṅkaḷūrk kiḻkaḻaniyil pullūrvatikku kiḻa
kkuk kiḻpāṟkellai vaṅkippuṟattu Attiyūrc cel
vakkiramavittan nilamāy Ivvūrt tirumeṟkoyilil
kolavāmanaāḻvārkku viṟṟukkuṭutta nilattukku meṟ
kum tenpāṟkellai Ēkatiravāykkālukku vaṭakkum
meypārkkellai pullūrvatikkuk kiḻakkum meṟkaṭai
ya nālumā nikki yataṉ kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟke
llai tiruppāccanallūr Ellaikkut teṟkum Āka Iṉ
nāṉkellaikku naṭuvu paḻampottakapaṭi ceṟu 16The continuation is lost.
Digital edition of SII 8.303 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Dorotea Operato.
